subtopics.subtopic_description: Educational background or training
notes: Holds an Associate Degree from a local community college
Degree focused on business administration and office technology
Received hands-on training in customer service and administrative software
Completed coursework in ethics, record management, and communication skills

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific job title and role description
notes: Official job title is Customer Service Representative at Planet Fitness
Primary duties include checking in members, promoting sales, and maintaining cleanliness
Acts as a frontline staff member responsible for member inquiries and assistance

subtopics.subtopic_description: Current industry or sector (e.g., tech, finance, manufacturing)
notes: Works in the fitness and recreation sector
Part of the customer service and membership management segment
Role aligns with municipal and license clerks in terms of administrative duties

subtopics.subtopic_description: Company size and environment
notes: Works at a mid-sized gym chain location, Planet Fitness
Club employs around 15-20 staff members per location
Work environment is fast-paced, customer-facing, and team-oriented

subtopics.subtopic_description: Type of business or market segment
notes: Planet Fitness operates in the fitness club and wellness industry
Business model focuses on affordable gym memberships and retail sales
Target market includes a broad demographic seeking accessible fitness options

subtopics.subtopic_description: Duration/years of experience in current role
notes: Has 1-2 years of experience as a customer service representative
Started working at Planet Fitness at age 19
Has completed at least one annual performance review in the current role

subtopics.subtopic_description: Professional seniority or career level
notes: Currently at an entry-level position in the company
No direct reports; works under supervision of club manager
Has begun to take on more responsibility as experience grows

subtopics.subtopic_description: Primary job responsibilities and regular daily tasks
notes: Performs daily club walks to restock sanitization stations and pick up items
Cleans and maintains the women's locker room, including deep cleaning
Checks in members using a POS and data tracking system
Handles member inquiries and promotes sales of gym merchandise
Monitors and maintains general cleanliness throughout the gym

subtopics.subtopic_description: Approximate proportion of time spent on core activities
notes: Spends about 40% of shift on cleaning and maintenance tasks
Member check-ins, inquiries, and sales promotions take up roughly 35% of time
Administrative and data entry tasks occupy 15% of daily work
Remaining 10% spent on restocking and miscellaneous duties

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of autonomy and scope of decision-making in the role
notes: Operates with moderate autonomy on routine cleaning and restocking decisions
Escalates unusual member issues or complaints to the manager
Can independently recommend merchandise or explain membership options
Follows established protocols for handling membership data and sales

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that feel easiest or most natural to perform
notes: Greeting and checking in members at the front desk
Restocking cleaning supplies and sanitization stations
Basic tidying and surface cleaning of gym areas

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks perceived as most challenging or complex
notes: Deep cleaning the women's locker room, especially during busy hours
Handling member complaints or unusual requests
Managing the POS system when there are multiple transactions or errors

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are repetitive, data-heavy, or suitable for automation
notes: Entering member check-in data into tracking systems
Restocking and inventory checks for cleaning supplies
Processing routine sales transactions at the POS terminal

subtopics.subtopic_description: Tasks that are most enjoyable or engaging versus those that feel boring or tedious
notes: Enjoys interacting with members and helping them with inquiries
Finds promoting sales and explaining membership benefits rewarding
Views repetitive cleaning tasks as less engaging, especially when traffic is low
Deep cleaning can feel tedious, especially if done solo for extended periods

subtopics.subtopic_description: Common pain points or inefficiencies in completing tasks
notes: Manual entry of member information can be time-consuming
Cleaning schedules can be disrupted by unexpected member traffic
Difficulty keeping track of inventory levels for supplies without automated alerts
POS system sometimes lags or requires multiple steps for simple transactions

subtopics.subtopic_description: How enjoyment, skill level, and productivity relate to one another
notes: Feels most productive and engaged when interacting directly with members
Tasks that match skill level, like check-ins, are completed faster and with fewer errors
Less enjoyable tasks can lead to slower completion and reduced attention to detail

subtopics.subtopic_description: Attitude towards learning new technologies and tools
notes: Generally open and curious about new tech that improves workflow
Sees value in tools that make daily tasks easier or more efficient
Prefers hands-on learning rather than theoretical instruction

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived adaptability to new software/methods
notes: Adapts quickly to user-friendly software with clear interfaces
Needs some initial guidance but gains confidence after practice
Comfortable with regular updates or new features if training is provided

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness to invest time in tech training
notes: Willing to set aside time for short, focused training sessions
Prefers training integrated into work shifts rather than outside hours
Motivated to learn if the new tool will noticeably reduce manual work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Motivations or barriers to learning new tech (e.g., workload, relevance)
notes: Primary motivation is making daily tasks more efficient
Barriers include high workload or lack of immediate relevance to current tasks
Sometimes hesitant if past tools have been difficult to use or unreliable

subtopics.subtopic_description: Influence of peers or management on willingness to adopt new tools
notes: Motivated by positive feedback from coworkers using new tech
More likely to adopt if management actively supports and provides resources
Peer demonstrations and team learning sessions increase confidence

topic: Primary Tools and Technologies Used in Work

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific software, platforms, or systems used daily
notes: Uses Data Server Track to monitor member check-ins and update records
Operates a POS system for processing payments and merchandise sales
Occasionally uses LLMs to generate responses or streamline member inquiries

subtopics.subtopic_description: Essential non-AI tools for workflow
notes: Relies on physical cleaning tools such as vacuums, mops, and dusters
Uses paper checklists for cleaning schedules and restocking duties
Utilizes storage closets stocked with cleaning and maintenance supplies

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with industry-standard technologies
notes: Familiar with POS systems commonly used in gym and retail settings
Understands basic data security practices for handling member information
Has exposure to scheduling and shift management software

subtopics.subtopic_description: Interoperability or integration issues between tools
notes: POS system and Data Server Track occasionally require redundant data entry
Member information updates are not always reflected immediately across all platforms
No automated sync between inventory tracking and cleaning supply ordering systems

subtopics.subtopic_description: Familiarity with fundamental AI/ML concepts and terminology
notes: Understands basic concepts like machine learning, natural language processing, and automation
Has used LLMs (large language models) for generating text and responses
Knows AI can be used for automating repetitive data entry and customer service tasks

subtopics.subtopic_description: Names of specific AI software/platforms currently used in work
notes: Uses ChatGPT or similar LLMs to help draft member communications
Applies AI-driven features in Data Server Track for report generation
No dedicated in-house AI platform, but integrates web-based AI tools as needed

subtopics.subtopic_description: Frequency and purpose of AI tool application (specific use cases)
notes: Uses LLMs at least once a week, primarily for customer communication templates
Applies AI to summarize member inquiries and streamline responses
Occasionally uses AI for quick knowledge lookups or process suggestions

subtopics.subtopic_description: Specific examples of AI success and failure experiences (lessons learned)
notes: AI suggested efficient solutions to recurring member issues, saving time
Encountered AI-generated responses that were too generic and needed manual edits
Learned that clear prompts yield better AI outputs
Experienced minor frustration when AI misunderstood nuanced gym policies

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of organizational training or peer resources for AI use
notes: No formal AI training programs provided by employer
Peers occasionally share tips for effective AI prompt writing
Relies on online resources and experimentation to learn new AI features

topic: AI Interaction Style and Workflow Change

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preferred mode of interaction: independent versus step-by-step collaboration
notes: Prefers step-by-step collaboration when first using new AI tools
Becomes more independent as familiarity with the system increases
Appreciates clear guidance or examples during initial learning

subtopics.subtopic_description: Style of human-AI teaming (e.g., advisor, assistant, co-worker)
notes: Views AI primarily as an assistant for repetitive tasks
Occasionally treats AI as an advisor for improving customer interactions
Relies on human judgment for final decisions, especially in customer-facing situations

subtopics.subtopic_description: Willingness and openness to adopting new AI-driven workflows
notes: Open to trying AI-driven workflows if they clearly reduce manual effort
Willing to adjust routines for tools that improve efficiency
Wants assurance that new workflows are reliable and easy to learn

subtopics.subtopic_description: Preference for conversational vs. command-based interfaces (communication dynamics)
notes: Prefers conversational interfaces for brainstorming or customer communication
Finds command-based interfaces faster for specific, repeatable tasks
Likes having the option to switch between modes depending on task complexity

subtopics.subtopic_description: Extent to which tasks rely on specialized, tacit domain knowledge
notes: Some tasks, like handling sensitive member complaints, require personal experience and judgment
Cleaning protocols are based on both standard procedures and situational awareness
Membership promotion and sales often benefit from understanding club culture and member preferences

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of trust in AI outputs for work tasks and critical decisions
notes: Trusts AI for routine communications and templated responses
Verifies AI suggestions before acting on them in complex or sensitive situations
Less likely to rely solely on AI for critical member account updates or disputes

subtopics.subtopic_description: Ideal balance of human effort and AI automation for specific tasks
notes: Prefers AI to automate repetitive data entry and report generation
Believes human oversight is necessary for member-facing and nuanced tasks
Supports a hybrid workflow where AI assists but final decisions remain human

subtopics.subtopic_description: Conditions under which high automation is acceptable or threatening
notes: Accepts high automation for back-office or inventory management processes
Finds high automation less acceptable for customer service or complaint resolution
Concerned about automation if it reduces meaningful human interactions with members

topic: AI Impact on Skills and Job Security

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived impact of AI on the importance of existing skills (enhanced vs. reduced)
notes: Sees AI as enhancing efficiency but not replacing the need for strong communication skills
Believes adaptability and tech literacy are becoming more important
Some routine skills, like manual data entry, are less critical due to automation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Emerging skills or new areas of responsibility created by AI
notes: Developing skills in AI prompt writing and output evaluation
Learning to troubleshoot or correct AI-generated errors
Taking on informal role of peer support for AI tool adoption

subtopics.subtopic_description: Level of concern about AI replacing specific tasks or the overall role
notes: Mildly concerned about automation of simple administrative tasks
Believes customer service roles will remain due to the need for human empathy
Watches industry trends but feels secure due to the people-oriented nature of the job

subtopics.subtopic_description: Availability of override mechanisms or manual checks for AI-driven processes
notes: Always reviews AI-generated member communications before sending
Manually corrects outputs from AI when they don't meet club standards
POS and data systems allow manual intervention if AI suggestions are incorrect

subtopics.subtopic_description: Perceived change in team or company policies regarding AI adoption
notes: Company is gradually more open to using AI as a support tool
No formal AI policy yet, but increased discussion among management
Team members encouraged to experiment with AI for productivity gains

subtopics.subtopic_description: General outlook on AI's broader societal and industry impact
notes: Believes AI can make routine jobs more efficient and reduce errors
Sees potential for AI to create new job categories in customer service
Concerned about potential job losses in highly repetitive administrative roles

subtopics.subtopic_description: Personal beliefs about the ethics and risks of AI in the workplace
notes: Thinks transparency in AI decision-making is essential
Wants AI tools to respect member privacy and data security
Believes over-reliance on AI could erode essential people skills

subtopics.subtopic_description: Missing AI tools or features that would be most beneficial in the future
notes: Would like AI to automate more of the check-in and sales process for members
Desires real-time inventory tracking and automated supply ordering
Wants smarter AI that can handle nuanced member requests without losing the personal touch

subtopics.subtopic_description: Predicted evolution of their job in the next 5-10 years with AI integration
notes: Expects more tasks to be automated, shifting the focus to member experience
Predicts need for stronger tech skills and adaptability
Believes the human element of customer service will remain central despite automation

subtopics.subtopic_description: Concrete steps they would want their organization to take regarding AI strategy
notes: Implement structured training for staff on effective AI use
Develop clear guidelines for when to use AI versus human judgment
Involve frontline employees in feedback and evaluation of new AI tools
